name: The Man/Angel
shortDescription: A human or angelic figure representing the element of Aquarius and intellect. Often depicted alongside other zodiacal symbols on cards like the Wheel of Fortune or the World.
description: The Man, often depicted as an angel or a mature human figure, is a recurring motif within the Tarot, most notably associated with the suit of Swords, and the zodiac sign of Aquarius. This figure symbolizes intellect, reason, and the power of thought. On cards like the Wheel of Fortune or the World, the Man appears alongside the Lion, Bull, and Eagle, representing the four fixed signs of the zodiac and the four elements. It embodies the rational mind and the ability to analyze and understand complex situations. The Man's presence suggests a need for careful consideration and thoughtful action. In some interpretations, the figure represents the higher self or spiritual guidance. The manifestation can also represent a messenger bringing important news or insights, similar to Hermes or Mercury in classical mythology. The figure's association with the element of Air reinforces its connection to intellect and communication, emphasizing the importance of clear thinking and objective analysis in achieving one's goals and navigating life's challenges.
appearance: A human or angelic figure, often winged, sometimes holding a book or scroll, representing knowledge and intellect. May be depicted as mature and wise, or youthful and energetic, depending on the card.

sources.title: The Pictorial Key to the Tarot
sources.author: A.E. Waite

sources.title: Meditations on the Tarot: A Journey into Christian Hermeticism
sources.author: Anonymous
